meaning: n.突进,突击,洪流,猛冲,(水的)奔流
examples: The onrush of tears took me by surprise.
She was screwing up her eyes against the onrush of air.
Technical onrush always is billow forward, vast and mighty, cannot hold back.
The onrush of "reality" shows (no actors, no scripts, just regular folks and countless unblinking cameras) sparked this subliminal blitz.
Looking ahead, the immediate outlook is clouded by the onrush of events.
